mappings.rationaleAI: Human evolution is directly addressed by this article on the human species and its development.
Prehistory encompasses the full span of human evolution from early hominins through the emergence of anatomically modern humans.
Africa is the continent where human evolution occurred, making it foundational to understanding human origins.
Human evolution traces the descent of Homo sapiens from primate ancestors through millions of years of natural selection.
Stone tool making is the earliest evidence of human technological capacity, dating back over 3 million years in hominin evolution.
The Stone Age encompasses the earliest period of human evolution and technological development.
Males represent half of the human species whose evolution is covered here.
Females represent half of the human species whose evolution is covered here.
Early human migration drove the global distribution of the species.
Human evolution traces the developmental stages of human life on Earth as covered here.
Primates include the human lineage whose evolution is traced in this section.
